excluded: a noisy-hardware robustness or auditability conclusion
evidence that any redesign candidate has passed
supported: pre-registered redesign plan after archiving the current IBM run and exhausting existing frozen lanes
fixed-width constraints and screening thresholds for future target/circuit redesign
hardware reopen criteria tied to IBM-informed primary noise rather than hand-built coherent-only wins

allowed_changes: phase component scaling before RY preparation
matched comparator packet regeneration with identical row/source surface
disallowed_changes: dropping matched RoPE-like, sinusoidal-like, ALIBI-like, or no-position controls
using hardware results for model selection
design_families.hypothesis: Reduce baseline score compression by centering PhaseWrap components in a balanced phase window before noisy readout.

allowed_changes: score contrast scaling bounded before count generation
new frozen real-source seed pairs before any hardware run
disallowed_changes: changing shot quantum thresholds after seeing results
design_families.hypothesis: Increase observable separation between PhaseWrap and positional comparators while preserving fixed-width score semantics.

allowed_changes: component-basis rotation in the classical packet freezer
matched transformation applied to all encoding families
IBM-informed simulated screening before packet lock
disallowed_changes: using provider-specific live results to tune packets
treating coherent-proxy-only wins as hardware-run approval
design_families.hypothesis: Choose component encodings whose PhaseWrap errors are less aligned with IBM readout and two-qubit stochastic shrinkage.

next_gate: Implement the first redesigned packet family and screen it under the Stage177 IBM-informed primary noise models before considering any hardware reopen.
objective: Determine whether PhaseWrap-RoPE's compact phase-wrap positional score has measurable robustness or auditability advantages on noisy quantum hardware, compared with matched positional-score encodings, under fixed circuit width.

screening_sequence.pass_condition: All candidate families produce product and CX packets with PhaseWrap, three positional comparators, and no-position control.
screening_sequence.purpose: Freeze redesigned fixed-width packet families before any provider action.

screening_sequence.pass_condition: PhaseWrap has stable strict wins on both templates under every primary IBM-informed model: ibm_backend_median_stochastic, ibm_backend_p75_stochastic
screening_sequence.purpose: Reject candidates that only win under hand-built coherent offsets.

screening_sequence.pass_condition: At least 2 independent real-source seed pairs pass the primary screen.
screening_sequence.purpose: Avoid one-seed artifacts before reopening hardware.

screening_sequence.pass_condition: Margins exceed 2.0 shot quanta over both best positional comparator and no-position control on at least 2 templates and 2 primary IBM-informed models.
screening_sequence.purpose: Reopen hardware only after simulation evidence justifies spend.
